title: Evangelion Home Video Database COMPLETED
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Home Video Database: COMPLETED!             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jun 28, 2011 5:36 am As some of you might know, I've been working on a complete database (with pictures) of all Japanese and North American home video releases of Evangelion. It's finally finished! Check it out here: http://svenge.pcriot.com/ I tested it with Firefox 5 and IE8, and since the tools I used significantly predate both browsers, there shouldn't be any compatibility issues. Let me know what you think!

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jun 28, 2011 5:47 am I use Firefox 5 and it works perfectly fine. I do not remember enough details to comment on the data accuracy but I love the neat layout without cluttering graphics or effects. And graphic images for each BD/DVD/LD/VHS are helpful (having VHS and LD information is cool). I also love the idea of FAQ with purchase information. Kudos for a very great job you have done for the fandom!! EDIT: It would be nice to have the actual Japanese text for the episode list...

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jun 28, 2011 6:07 am symbv wrote: It would be nice to have the actual Japanese text for the episode list...

[]:             Quote Post by Viking » Tue Jun 28, 2011 6:17 am It's quite interesting. I'll look at it better later because I'm short of time now. Anyway, from a merely technical point of view ( note that this an advice rather than a criticism ), you should have avoided frames. I'd recommend you using CSS instead, to create that kind of "navigation bar". Also, if you plan to put the Japanese titles as symbv suggested, be careful with the encoding declarations of the document. Use the W3C validator as much as you can because is one of the web developers' best friends . Anyway, it's a very good job. Well done! Bye

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jun 28, 2011 6:34 am Viking wrote: It's quite interesting. I'll look at it better later because I'm short of time now. Anyway, from a merely technical point of view ( note that this an advice rather than a criticism ), you should have avoided frames. I'd recommend you using CSS instead, to create that kind of "navigation bar".

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Tue Jun 28, 2011 7:39 am The site is okay on Chrome, and speaking of editing tools, get Notepad++, which is a freeware ASCII editor with a punch.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jun 28, 2011 9:00 am One thing I noticed is that you don't mention that the original release of Platinum volume 1 was missing dialogue and that you could request a replacement disc: http://www.evamonkey.com/platinum-volume-1-missing-dialogue/

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jun 28, 2011 9:29 am The Eva Monkey wrote: One thing I noticed is that you don't mention that the original release of Platinum volume 1 was missing dialogue and that you could request a replacement disc: http://www.evamonkey.com/platinum-volume-1-missing-dialogue/

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jun 28, 2011 9:55 am Yeah, I'm aware. Just wanted to point out to you that technically, like with the original DVD release, there exist two versions of volume 1. If it helps, I found a thread on Mania (formerly AnimeOnDVD) that might be helpful. http://www.mania.com/aodvb/archive/index.php/t-55466.html Good work on this, dude.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jun 28, 2011 9:56 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Yeah, I'm aware. Just wanted to point out to you that technically, like with the original DVD release, there exist two versions of volume 1.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jun 28, 2011 10:04 am svenge wrote: I'm not sure an exchange is possible anymore, considering what's happened to ADV Films.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jun 28, 2011 10:28 am symbv wrote: What happened to ADV films? Closed down?

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Tue Jun 28, 2011 10:56 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Technically, they fractured and rebranded into several smaller companies that don't really do a whole lot in the grander scheme of things. ADV's website still has the press release which explains the break up. http://www.advfilms.com/ Not knowing a whole lot about business, it comes off as reincorporating to shed debt, but they've repeatedly stated that they are NOT the same company anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 28, 2011 11:24 am Yay, now I don't have to refer to this site anymore for LD information! Seriously though, good job, it's great to have resources like this on hand. But I think a banner or some kind of background image would be a nice tough. (In Chrome, it's an all white background.)

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jun 28, 2011 11:41 am ^ It is white background on Firefox too, but I really like it plain there.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jun 28, 2011 11:41 am The source code makes me twitch a little.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jun 28, 2011 11:57 am The Eva Monkey wrote: The source code makes me twitch a little.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jun 28, 2011 12:03 pm svenge wrote: I've never cared for fancy backgrounds and/or white text on black, so I decided to have plain text with nothing distracting from it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 28, 2011 12:14 pm svenge wrote: I've never cared for fancy backgrounds and/or white text on black, so I decided to have plain text with nothing distracting from it. That, and it's less work to do it that way.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jun 28, 2011 12:42 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I've been attempting to make an Eva site of my own for a fan-project of mine, so I know how this goes.
